articles.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Friday
articles.content: Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex ended largely unchanged as financial stocks, led by HDFC Bank, offset gains in auto and FMCG shares. Analysts flagged weak momentum, bearish technical signals and key support near 25,400, indicating choppy trade and possible near-term downside.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Friday

articles.title: Home First Finance bulk deal alert: Promoters sell stake worth Rs 660 crore; Societe Generale among buyers
articles.content: Home First Finance shares fell nearly 6% after promoters Aether and True North sold Rs 660 crore stake via bulk deals to Societe Generale and PICTET. Despite the decline, the stock has outperformed benchmarks over one year and posted strong quarterly profit growth.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Home First Finance bulk deal alert: Promoters sell stake worth Rs 660 crore; Societe Generale among buyers

articles.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Data Patterns and Finolex Cables on Friday for short term gains
articles.content: The Nifty ended flat as financial stocks dragged despite gains in auto and consumer sectors. Technical indicators signal continued weakness, with resistance at 25,550–25,600 and downside risk toward 25,300. Analysts recommend buying Data Patterns and Finolex Cables on bullish breakouts.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Market Trading Guide: Buy Data Patterns and Finolex Cables on Friday for short term gains

articles.title: Sebi warns investors against fake STT notices, flags market scams
articles.content: Sebi said it has observed cases where entities impersonate its officials, misuse names and designations, and create fake email IDs resembling those of the regulator.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi warns investors against fake STT notices, flags market scams

articles.title: Sebi mandates mutual funds value gold, silver using domestic prices
articles.content: In a circular issued on Thursday, the market regulator said mutual funds will now value physical gold and silver held by schemes using polled spot prices published by stock exchanges. nbsp;
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi mandates mutual funds value gold, silver using domestic prices

articles.title: Sebi tightens social media norms, mandates registration disclosures
articles.content: Sebi said the objective is to distinguish content posted by registered entities from that of unregistered individuals, thereby strengthening investor protection and promoting ease of doing business.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi tightens social media norms, mandates registration disclosures

articles.title: Sebi’s new MF rulebook: Clarity or more clutter?
articles.content: Sebi's new categorisation circular fixes old problems while handing the industry fresh material for the next round of product clutter
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi’s new MF rulebook: Clarity or more clutter?

articles.title: NSE CEO pitches for minimum qualifying criteria for derivatives trading
articles.content: NSE's managing director and CEO Ashishkumar Chauhan on Thursday made a case for 'minimum qualifying criteria' for those participating in derivatives trading to prevent people from lower strata of society from wasting their money on speculation.
articles.primarySourceTitle: NSE CEO pitches for minimum qualifying criteria for derivatives trading

articles.title: Sebi mandates market intermediaries to disclose registration details on social media from May 1
articles.content: Sebi has directed all regulated entities and their agents to display registered names and registration numbers on social media platforms. The move aims to enhance transparency, curb misleading content and help investors distinguish between regulated intermediaries and unregistered market participants online.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi mandates market intermediaries to disclose registration details on social media from May 1

articles.title: Sebi caps mutual fund overlap, forces schemes to truly differ
articles.content: In a circular issued on Thursday, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) capped portfolio overlaps at 50% for sectoral and thematic equity schemes with other equity schemes within the same fund house, barring large-cap funds.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi caps mutual fund overlap, forces schemes to truly differ

articles.title: Sebi Working Groups submit agri derivatives review reports to chairman
articles.content: Working groups constituted by Sebi to review the delivery and settlement framework and existing regulatory norms in the agricultural commodity derivatives segment have submitted their reports to Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey, the regulator said on Thursday.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Sebi Working Groups submit agri derivatives review reports to chairman

articles.title: Stock market quote of the day by John Bogle | "Don't look for the needle in the haystack. Just buy the haystack!"
articles.content: John Bogle's philosophy advocates for owning the entire market through index funds rather than attempting to pick individual winning stocks. This approach mitigates the difficulty of stock picking, reduces company-specific risk through diversification, and combats the silent wealth destroyer of high costs. It offers a behavioral advantage, promo...

articles.title: Indian bonds fall ahead of debt auction, growth data
articles.content: Indian government bonds ​ended lower on Thursday after a ​choppy session, as traders looked ahead to the penultimate ​debt auction of the current financial year and the first GDP growth data under a new series.

articles.title: Sebi unveils life-cycle mutual funds; solution-oriented schemes scrapped — what it means for your portfolio
articles.content: Mutual funds will now be permitted to offer both value and contra mutual funds as long as the scheme portfolio overlap between the two schemes is not more than 50%.

articles.title: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery IPO sails through on Day 3: Check subscription status, GMP and other key details
articles.content: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery's IPO was fully subscribed on February 26, receiving bids for 70,31,776 shares, 1.23 times the offer. The employee segment was oversubscribed 6.74 times, while the allotment finalisation is on February 27.

articles.title: ‘Death of Software?’ Anand Mahindra calls AI doom ‘Greatly Exaggerated’ amid IT stock crash
articles.content: Indian IT stocks face sharp corrections amid AI disruption fears. Anand Mahindra advises against extreme AI doomsday predictions. He believes AI will pressure IT firms to adapt, focusing on outcomes and value delivery. While some roles may change, new opportunities in AI integration and governance will emerge.

articles.title: Market Wrap: Nifty, Sensex end flat in a volatile trade as heavyweight HDFC Bank drags market
articles.content: Indian benchmark indices Nifty and BSE Sensex ended flat on Thursday, weighed down by selling in financial stocks led by HDFC Bank. Market sentiment stayed weak as technical indicators signalled fading momentum, while global cues remained mixed across major Asian and European indices.

articles.title: Sensex slips 27 points, Nifty 50 rises 14 points— 10 key highlights from the Indian stock market today
articles.content: The Sensex ended 27 points, or 0.03%, lower at 82,248.61, while the Nifty 50 inched up by 14 points, or 0.06%, to settle at 25,496.55. The BSE 150 MidCap Index clocked a decent gain of 0.55%, while the BSE 250 SmallCap Index rose by 0.07%.

articles.title: Bank of Japan interest rate decision in focus: Will BOJ tighten policy? How it could impact emerging markets like India
articles.content: As speculation grows about potential interest rate hikes by the Bank of Japan, experts weigh in on how these changes could impact emerging markets, particularly India. In December, BOJ raised its key policy rate by 25 basis points to 0.75%, which is its highest level since September 1995.

articles.title: Nifty no longer range-bound? Vinay Rajani sees upside break with 2 high-conviction trades
articles.content: After a month of indecision, derivative signals and FII positioning suggest a Nifty breakout is imminent, according to Vinay Rajani of HDFC Securities. articles.title: How much gold is enough? Ray Dalio reveals ideal allocation for your portfolio; find out here
articles.content: Ray Dalio emphasizes gold's value during instability, citing historical performance during the 1970s and 1930s crises. Gold should comprise 5-15% of portfolios as it acts as a stabilizer and diversifier, protecting wealth when traditional assets struggle amid economic uncertainties.
articles.primarySourceTitle: How much gold is enough? Ray Dalio reveals ideal allocation for your portfolio; find out here

articles.title: Grade-A office spaces in a sweet spot, but risks loom

articles.title: Gold, silver soared in 2025, stocks were major drags. What could 2026 have in store for investors?
articles.content: After gold and silver delivered spectacular gains in 2025, Indian equities enter 2026 shaped by slower global rate cuts, geopolitical risks and a shift from slowdown to recovery. Largecaps may stay resilient, while mid and smallcaps could benefit from improving consumption trends and banking reforms, though global growth and currency risks persi...

articles.title: Auto sales preview: Strong retail demand to drive double-digit wholesale growth in February: Report

articles.title: New RBI curbs on prop funding could give foreign traders an edge
articles.content: Amended capital market exposure rules aimed at curbing bank lending for speculative purposes could raise costs for domestic proprietary traders, benefiting their global counterparts.

articles.title: PSU banks better placed on loan-deposit metrics; microfinance cycle nearing normalisation, says Yuvraj Choudhary
articles.content: Public sector banks are showing resilience in their loan-to-deposit ratios, with a significant gap compared to private peers. articles.title: Time to increase exposure to equities; remain overweight on mid, small-caps, says Mihir Vora of TRUST Mutual Fund
articles.content: Mihir Vora of TRUST Mutual Fund believes this is the right time to increase equity exposure, anticipating healthy earnings growth. Vora highlights that he has been consistently overweight in mid- and small-caps due to greater growth opportunities in these segments.

articles.title: Nifty 50 enters March series with lower OI; FII shorts reduce sharply
articles.content: Nifty’s February series rollover stood at 68.2%, falling below the previous expiry’s 70.8% and the three- and six-month averages of 70.6% and 75.6%, as the March series commenced with 142.73 lakh shares in open interest (OI), reflecting a decline of 31.54 lakh shares despite a 1% rise in prices.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Nifty 50 enters March series with lower OI; FII shorts reduce sharply

articles.title: Turnaround Play: 6 stocks turn profitable in Q3, rally up to 55% in just 3 months
articles.content: Twenty-seven BSE-listed companies with market caps over Rs 3,000 crore turned profitable sequentially in the December quarter. While six stocks saw share price surges of 10-55%, many others declined, with the top five laggards dropping 20-40% in three months.

articles.title: HAL, BEL and other defence stocks jump up to 5% on potential India-Israel defence deal
articles.content: Defence stocks such as Hindustan Aeronautics, Bharat Electronics and others gained up to 5% after reports of a potential India–Israel defence pact. The agreement, expected during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Tel Aviv visit, may prioritise technology transfer in missile defence and advanced weapon systems.

articles.title: Accord Transformer Switchgear allotment to be finalised today: Step-by-step guide to check your allotment status
articles.content: Accord Transformer  amp; Switchgear's IPO allotment is expected today, following a 357 times subscription. Retail investors will receive shares proportionally, with refunds starting February 27. The IPO may list on BSE SME on March 02, with an 8 per share grey market premium.

articles.title: IT sector valuations turn attractive after AI sell-off: Emkay; Adds Infosys, HCL Tech; Exits Mphasis in portfolio reset
articles.content: In its model portfolio, Emkay Global has added Indus Towers, SRF, Infosys, and HCL Technologies, while exiting Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, Gravita India and Mphasis. It has trimmed its positions in Maruti Suzuki India, Larsen  amp; Toubro (L amp;T), and Craftsman Automation.

articles.title: Angel One share price crashes 90% today? Here’s what actually happened
articles.content: Following a 1:10 stock split, Angel One shares fell nearly 90% on Thursday, opening at  ₹251.35. Despite a profit decline in Q3 FY26, total income rose. The split aims to enhance liquidity while keeping overall ownership value consistent for shareholders.

articles.title: IT will outlast AI hype, private banks beat PSUs: Ajay Tyagi's contrarian playbook

articles.title: IIT professor's entrepreneurial bet pays Rs 1,000 crore, students too strike IPO riches
articles.content: An entrepreneurial leap by IIT-Bombay professor Shashikanth Suryanarayanan has turned into a windfall as his stake in Sedemac Mechatronics is valued at nearly Rs 1,000 crore ahead of its IPO. Former students-turned-cofounders are also set to reap significant gains as the deeptech firm heads to the market.
